title: Tryde Coffee Table
summary: A 2x6 plank-top coffee table with 2x4 breadboard ends and 4x4 legs on a simple apron base — a fast, sturdy build with minimal parts.
description:
This coffee table is a genuinely quick build: a four-board 2x6 tabletop capped with breadboard ends, sitting on a basic 4x4-leg-and-2x4-apron base. Cut all four tabletop boards to exactly matching length before anything else — a mismatched board here is the one mistake that shows on every side of the finished top.

Drill pocket holes on the ends of all the tabletop boards, then join three of them edge to edge before capping both ends with the breadboard pieces — measuring the actual assembled width for the breadboard cuts rather than trusting a plan number, since edge-glued boards rarely land on an exact calculated width. **Avoid overtightening the pocket hole screws** on the tabletop joints specifically — the plan calls this out directly, since overdriving them can bow the top inward rather than holding it flat.

Build the two end-apron assemblies identically, then the longer side aprons the same way, before centering the whole base on the underside of the finished tabletop and screwing up through the base's pre-drilled, upward-facing pocket holes. Five total steps, four legs, one tabletop — there's very little here to get lost in.

materials.name: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

cutList.note: Cut to fit your actual assembled tabletop width.

steps.title: Build the tabletop
steps.body: Cut the four tabletop boards to exactly matching length. Drill pocket holes on the ends of all boards, and on three of them drill pocket holes for edge-to-edge attachment. Join edge to edge with 2-1/2" pocket hole screws and glue, avoiding overtightening the screws to prevent the top from bowing inward.

steps.title: Attach the breadboard ends
steps.body: Measure the actual assembled width of the tabletop and cut the breadboard ends to match. Attach through the predrilled pocket holes with 2-1/2" pocket hole screws.

steps.title: Build the end assemblies
steps.body: Drill 1-1/2" pocket holes in the end aprons, including holes facing upward for attaching the tabletop later. Attach to the legs. Build two identical assemblies.

steps.title: Attach the side aprons
steps.body: Repeat the same process for the longer side aprons, joining the two end assemblies into a complete base.

steps.title: Attach the base to the top and finish
steps.body: Center the base on the underside of the tabletop and attach through the predrilled pocket holes with 2-1/2" pocket hole screws. Sand and finish.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

unresolvedImages.alt: Coffee table with a 2x6 plank top, breadboard ends, and 4x4 legs
